paper.title: Pooling by Sliced-Wasserstein Embedding
paper.abstract: Learning representations from sets has become increasingly important with many applications in point cloud processing, graph learning, image/video recognition, and object detection. We introduce a geometrically-interpretable and generic pooling mechanism for aggregating a set of features into a fixed-dimensional representation. In particular, we treat elements of a set as samples from a probability distribution and propose an end-to-end trainable Euclidean embedding for sliced-Wasserstein distance to learn from set-structured data effectively. We evaluate our proposed pooling method on a wide variety of set-structured data, including point-cloud, graph, and image classification tasks, and demonstrate that our proposed method provides superior performance over existing set representation learning approaches. Our code is available at https://github.com/navid-naderi/PSWE.

review_fields.comment: I acknowledge the author's efforts to respond to my questions; I increase my rating slightly. However, my concerns are not fully addressed: 1. Modelnet40 is not a large dataset and I think it's more convincing to demonstrate the practical value by reaching a reasonable performance on this dataset. 2. The scale of the experiments in the 2D vision domain is also too small. That said I am mainly concerned the practical value of the this work.
